  • Intersectional approaches
  • Inclusive design
  • Community empowerment
  • Educational interventions
  • Positive digital engagement models

Democracy Hackathon Challenges

Participants must choose one of four official challenge tracks.

1. Privacy-Preserving Hate Speech Detection Challenge (PrivHSD)

Mentored by researchers from the Technical University of Munich.

Objective:

Build hate speech detection systems that:

  • Maintain strong privacy protections
  • Operate independently of user identity
  • Avoid harmful surveillance outcomes

Expected Outputs:

  • Prototype
  • Demonstrable concept
  • Research note
  • Privacy-preserving architecture

2. Coordinated Inauthentic Behaviour Identification

Mentored by the Institute for Strategic Dialogue.

Objective:

Create tools capable of:

  • Detecting coordinated online campaigns
  • Tracking hate narratives across platforms
  • Identifying gendered harassment campaigns

Potential Deliverables:

  • Monitoring dashboard
  • Narrative clustering systems
  • Visualized analytical tools

3. Reporting and Response Systems for Online Hate

Mentored by Democracy Reporting International.

Objective:

Develop systems that:

  • Transform online reports into structured evidence
  • Support electoral commissions
  • Improve referral and escalation pathways

Expected Outputs:

  • MVPs
  • Wireframes
  • Referral systems
  • Classification frameworks

4. Think Out of the Box Challenge

Mentored by the University of Strasbourg.

Objective:

Create transformative approaches that:

  • Prevent hate speech
  • Contextualize harmful content
  • Promote empathy and dialogue
  • Empower communities

Potential Solutions:

  • Educational tools
  • Social impact platforms
  • Policy frameworks
  • Moderation interfaces
  • Creative interventions

Who Should Apply?

The hackathon is designed for multidisciplinary teams of 3–4 members.

Ideal teams should include:

1. Technologist / Backend Developer

Responsible for:

  • AI systems
  • NLP tools
  • Detection algorithms
  • Technical implementation

2. Democracy or Policy Expert

Responsible for:

  • Human rights alignment
  • Democratic frameworks
  • Governance insights
  • Ethical guidance

3. Designer / UX/UI Innovator

Responsible for:

  • User experience
  • Interface design
  • Accessibility
  • Visualization

Teams may also add a fourth member if needed.

Gender balance within teams is strongly encouraged.

How to Apply

Step 1: Form a Team

Applicants must create a team of:

  • 3–4 members
  • Diverse skill backgrounds

Step 2: Submit a Concept Summary

Teams must prepare a concept summary of no more than two pages.

The proposal should include:

  • Problem statement
  • Proposed solution
  • Human rights considerations
  • Technical overview
  • Expected impact
  • User experience strategy

Step 3: Selection and Warm-Up Session

Selected teams will:

  • Join a virtual preparation session
  • Refine their ideas with mentors
  • Prepare for the live Strasbourg event

Apply: Click Here to learn more and apply.

Important Dates

Application Deadline:

5 June 2026

Event Dates:

17–19 June 2026

Location:

Palais de l’Europe, Strasbourg, France

What Makes This Opportunity Unique?

Unlike traditional hackathons focused only on coding, the Democracy Hackathon combines:

  • Technology
  • Human rights
  • Democracy
  • Ethics
  • Public policy
  • Social innovation

Participants are not just building apps — they are designing tools that could influence the future of democratic governance and digital safety across Europe.

This hackathon offers a rare opportunity to:

  • Collaborate internationally
  • Engage with policymakers
  • Receive expert mentorship
  • Build impactful civic technology
  • Gain visibility in the democracy and human-rights space

For young innovators passionate about democracy, AI ethics, digital rights, and social impact, this is one of the most meaningful technology competitions of 2026.

Discover more competition opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Democracy Hackathon 2026 in Strasbourg, France | Fully Funded Opportunity to Build Tech Solutions Against Online Hate appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ETH Zürich PhD Scholarship 2026 in Agricultural Economics and Climate Policy in Switzerland | Fully Funded 4-Year Doctoral Opportunity
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
ETH Zürich has officially announced a fully funded PhD Scholarship opportunity for students interested in Agricultural Economics, Climate Policy, Sustainability, and Climate Change Mitigation in Swiss agriculture. The doctoral position is hosted within the Agricultural Economics and Policy Group led by Professor Robert Finger and forms part of the major national research initiative known as […]

The post ETH Zürich PhD Scholarship 2026 in Agricultural Economics and Climate Policy in Switzerland | Fully Funded 4-Year Doctoral Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
ETH Zürich has officially announced a fully funded PhD Scholarship opportunity for students interested in Agricultural Economics, Climate Policy, Sustainability, and Climate Change Mitigation in Swiss agriculture.

The doctoral position is hosted within the Agricultural Economics and Policy Group led by Professor Robert Finger and forms part of the major national research initiative known as the ACHIEVE Project: “Pathways to a Net-Zero Target for Hard-to-Abate Swiss Emissions.”

This prestigious four-year doctoral opportunity offers researchers the chance to work on one of the most urgent global challenges: reducing greenhouse gas emissions in agri-food systems while developing practical and evidence-based climate policy solutions.

The position is based in Zürich, Switzerland, and provides:

  • Full funding for four years
  • Competitive ETH Zürich salary
  • Research and training opportunities
  • International collaboration
  • Strong academic mentorship
  • Career development support

The application deadline is 26 June 2026.

About ETH Zürich

ETH Zürich is internationally recognized as one of the world’s leading universities in science, technology, sustainability, and innovation.

The university is known for:

  • Cutting-edge research
  • Academic excellence
  • Global scientific impact
  • Innovation-driven education
  • Interdisciplinary collaboration

ETH Zürich hosts over 30,000 students and researchers from more than 120 countries and consistently ranks among the top universities globally.

The institution is committed to:

  • Sustainability
  • Climate neutrality
  • Equal opportunity
  • Diversity and inclusion
  • Independent and innovative thinking

Its research environment encourages collaboration across academia, industry, government, and civil society to solve major global challenges.

About the ACHIEVE Project

The PhD scholarship is funded under the national research initiative:

“Pathways to a Net-Zero Target for Hard-to-Abate Swiss Emissions” (ACHIEVE)

The ACHIEVE project brings together:

  • 29 member entities
  • 27 collaboration partners
  • Researchers
  • Public sector institutions
  • Industry actors
  • Civil society stakeholders
  • Policymakers

The initiative focuses on developing:

  • Net-zero emission strategies for Switzerland
  • Climate mitigation pathways
  • Evidence-based policy recommendations
  • Sustainable transition frameworks

Within this project, the Agricultural Economics and Policy Group at ETH Zürich leads a work package focused on reducing greenhouse gas emissions within Swiss agri-food systems.

Research Focus of the PhD Position

The doctoral student will conduct research on:

  • Climate change mitigation in agriculture
  • Agricultural economics and policy
  • Behavioural responses to climate policy
  • Sustainable agri-food systems
  • Farmer decision-making processes

The project specifically investigates the:

  • Economic aspects of climate mitigation
  • Policy effectiveness and efficiency
  • Behavioural dimensions of environmental action
  • Impact of climate policies on Swiss agriculture

Key Research Areas

The selected PhD candidate will work on topics such as:

  • Greenhouse gas emission reduction strategies
  • Farmer behaviour and climate adaptation
  • Agricultural policy instruments
  • Climate-related decision-making
  • Sustainability transitions in agriculture
  • Data-driven agricultural policy analysis
  • Economic incentives for environmental action

Research Methodology and Activities

The doctoral project combines:

  • Economic theory
  • Data collection
  • Survey research
  • Behavioural experiments
  • Quantitative analysis
  • Stakeholder engagement

Major Research Activities Include:

1. Conducting Surveys and Choice Experiments

The candidate will:

  • Design and implement farmer surveys
  • Conduct behavioural choice experiments
  • Gather primary data from Swiss farmers

2. Data Analysis

Responsibilities include:

  • Analyzing survey data
  • Working with secondary datasets
  • Conducting economic and statistical analysis

3. Stakeholder Interaction

The researcher will engage with:

  • Farmers
  • Policymakers
  • Agricultural stakeholders
  • Sustainability experts

4. Collaboration with Researchers

The candidate will collaborate with:

  • PhD students
  • Postdoctoral researchers
  • International project teams
  • Interdisciplinary research groups

5. Student Supervision

The doctoral student will also:

  • Supervise bachelor students
  • Support master’s students

PhD Training and Academic Development

As part of the doctoral trajectory, the selected candidate will:

  • Complete 18 ECTS of PhD-level coursework
  • Participate in advanced academic training
  • Engage in interdisciplinary learning opportunities

ETH Zürich provides extensive mentoring and professional development support throughout the program.

Duration and Start Date

Duration:

  • Fully funded for four years

Employment Type:

  • Full-time
  • Fixed-term position

Planned Start Period:

  • Last quarter of 2026 (October–December)

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet the following criteria.

Educational Qualification

Candidates should hold:

  • A Master’s degree in Agricultural Economics, or
  • A closely related discipline

Applicants close to completing their Master’s degree may also apply.

Language Requirements

Because the project involves direct engagement with Swiss farmers and stakeholders, applicants must demonstrate:

  • C1 proficiency in German or French

Strong English communication and academic writing skills are also highly beneficial.

Skills and Competencies

Applicants should possess:

  • Strong analytical skills
  • Quantitative research abilities
  • Academic writing competence
  • Interest in climate policy and sustainability
  • Ability to work in interdisciplinary environments

Benefits of the ETH Zürich PhD Scholarship

ETH Zürich offers one of the most attractive doctoral research environments in Europe.

Key Benefits Include:

1. Fully Funded Position

  • Competitive salary according to ETH Zürich standards
  • Full-time funding for four years

2. Research Excellence

  • Access to world-class research infrastructure
  • Collaboration with leading researchers

3. Personalized Mentorship

  • Strong academic supervision
  • Career guidance and mentoring

4. International and Diverse Environment

  • Multicultural research community
  • Interdisciplinary collaboration opportunities

5. Career Development Support

The program prepares graduates for careers in:

  • Academia
  • Government
  • Industry
  • International organizations
  • Sustainability policy institutions

6. Continuous Training Opportunities

  • PhD-level courses
  • Skills development workshops
  • Academic networking opportunities

ETH Zürich’s Commitment to Diversity and Sustainability

ETH Zürich strongly promotes:

  • Equality of opportunity
  • Inclusive academic culture
  • Respect for diversity
  • Sustainability-focused research

The university encourages applicants from diverse backgrounds and fosters a research environment where all staff and students can thrive.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit the following as a single PDF:

  1. Cover letter
  2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  3. Contact details of two referees
  4. Academic transcripts

Application Deadline

Deadline: 26 June 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply early and carefully prepare strong research-oriented application materials.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should:

  1. Visit the ETH Zürich online application portal
  2. Prepare all required documents
  3. Submit the application online before the deadline

Important:

  • Applications submitted by email or post will not be accepted

Apply HERE

Why This Opportunity Is Important

Climate change mitigation in agriculture is one of the most pressing global policy challenges today. Agriculture plays a critical role in greenhouse gas emissions, biodiversity protection, food security, and sustainability transitions.

This ETH Zürich PhD Scholarship provides researchers with the opportunity to:

  • Contribute to impactful climate research
  • Influence future agricultural policies
  • Work directly with stakeholders
  • Develop practical sustainability solutions
  • Build a globally respected academic career

For students passionate about sustainability, agricultural economics, climate governance, and environmental policy, this doctoral opportunity represents an exceptional pathway into high-impact international research.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post ETH Zürich PhD Scholarship 2026 in Agricultural Economics and Climate Policy in Switzerland | Fully Funded 4-Year Doctoral Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
